author: @BurntKittyForge-pl8ol
text: ...the Automotive air conditioner pulley mounted clutch could energize off the induction it produces alone and then change gears in high speed [edit: another form similar to centrifugal operation but easier to control , maybe more simple too]  , for example so the blades don't over speed their limit and still it would produce more power possibly ...it's an interesting type clutch for push button operation also  , which is  possible with a cable clutch too but it'd involve more parts was all , hard to explain briefly sometimes

author: @BurntKittyForge-pl8ol
text: I should check out your channels more , some new videos everyday looks like...I'd like a 3d printer if I could justify it and I see more items on eBay for example 3d printed, I mention clutches sometimes as a tinker daydream component and I've been reaping a pulley on a car AC unit that was seized and breaking the serpentine belt , the odd thing it uses a magnetic clutch but I don't trust the new parts on the old compressor , so I'm studying disconnect the clutch I just installed and was switching parts really so now i'n fascinated how the magnetic clutch on the pulley activates , it's odd trying to study how to make it not turn on the fuse controls the heater, fan and more , finally I find out "MG relay" is for magnetic clutch after learning also a relay can get stuck ON , (not my problem but I plan to disconnect that one so there's no strain from the compressor ...srry too long rant but the part is interesting , I was interested in belt driven components for example the Auto AC could run off spinning motion alone in the past but this is clutch related , could be electronically combined with an RPM meter gauge as well...

author: @kennethbeal
text: I am working on donating a 3D printer to the nearby school/church.  I think this is the one.  Thank you!!!

replies.author: @theaussieviking8555
replies.text:
A very honorable thought. I donate my older printers to special needs children so their parents can guide them.

The only thing that comes to mind though is that Multi colour printers are more complex and things can go wrong where knowledge is needed to fix the issues. Not a dealbreaker if it's overseen by someone with knowledge to do so. But if not a reliable single colour has less to go wrong and they can always learn to paint/airbrush their prints.

I have the Bambu Carbon X1 and the 5 Toolhead Prusa XL and have also had a few other multi colours prior to that such as Prusa's MMU2 and the Mosaic Palette 2 and there is a steeper learning curve than "just 3D". So perhaps discuss this with the intended recipients in case they would prefer simplicity to start with.

author: @DrB934
text: Maybe the PTFE tube inserts on the orange clip spool retainers are just a cheap way to reduce friction on the sides of the spools as they turn during printing.

replies.author: @theaussieviking8555
replies.text: Personally, I think it's a handy spot to push the end of the filament to prevent unravelling when unloading and changing spools. I've had my AD5X since the 26th of January.

author: @ernestsamec
text:
discovering your channel was a revelation. You have this incredible gift for making science feel simple and doable—something I never thought possible. For years now (honestly, I’ve lost count!), your videos have inspired me to roll up my sleeves and try things myself. And it’s been life-changing. Because of you, I’ve tinkered with graphene, built batteries, hacked together generators, and even turned my old room into a lab. I yes I blame you for the mess, It wasn't there before i found your channel. Coincidence? I think not😂😂

The way you explain and demonstrate these complex topics has unlocked a world I didn’t know I could access. You’ve taught me that curiosity isn’t just for experts; it’s for anyone willing to try. So thank you, not just for the knowledge, but for showing us what’s possible when we stop doubting and start doing.
Please never stop making the videos you love. What you share matters more than you know.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: mate! - thank you very much for taking the time to write that - i can tell it came from the heart - I very much appreciate it - cheers and all the best to you as you explore my friend

replies.author: @JSfuckgoogle
replies.text:
Been watching for many years now.. there is NO-ONE quite like Robert! 😎

Knowledgeable, insightful, a "walker, not a talker," genuinely uplifting, has that classic "can do attitude" and even moreso 100% genuine!

A must watch, in a sea of complete & utter ego fuelled dross! Lol
